segments.text: In the meantime so many thousands of people had gathered that they were stepping on each other. Jesus began speaking first to his disciples. {
Beware the yeast of the Pharisees—hypocrisy.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For there's nothing hidden that won't be revealed, nothing secret that won't be made known.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whatever you have said in the dark will be heard in the light, and whatever you whispered in private will be announced from the rooftops.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, my friends, don't be afraid of those who kill the body, for once they've done that there's no more they can do.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Let me make it clear whom you should be afraid of. You should be afraid of the one who after he has killed has the power to dispose of them in Gehenna. [^50] That's the one you should be afraid of.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Aren't five sparrows sold for two pennies? But God doesn't forget a single one of them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Even the hairs on your head have been counted. Don't be afraid—you're worth more than many sparrows!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you the truth, those who declare they belong to me, the Son of man will also declare they belong to him before God's angels,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but those who deny me will be denied before God's angels.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Everyone who speaks against the Son of man will be forgiven, but anyone who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When you're brought to trial before synagogues, rulers, and authorities, don't worry about how to defend yourself, or what you should say.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Holy Spirit will teach you at that time what's important to say.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Someone in the crowd asked Jesus, {
Teacher, please tell my brother to share the inheritance with me.
segments.speaker: Someone in the crowd

segments.text: My friend,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,
Who appointed me as your judge to decide how your inheritance should be divided?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He told the people,
Watch out, and beware of all greedy thoughts and actions, for a person's life isn't summed up by all the things they own.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then he told them a story as an illustration. {
Once there was a rich man who owned land that was very productive.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After thinking things through, the man said to himself, ‘What shall I do? I've nowhere to store my crops.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I know what I'll do,’ he decided. ‘I'll pull down my barns and build bigger ones, and then I'll be able to store all my crops and everything I own.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then I'll tell myself: You have enough to live on for many years, so take life easy: eat, drink, and enjoy yourself!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But God said to him, ‘You foolish man! Your life will be demanded back this very night, and then who will get everything you've stored up?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This is what happens to people who hoard up wealth for themselves but are not rich as far as God is concerned.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus told his disciples, {
That's why I tell you don't worry about life, about what to eat, or about what clothes you should wear.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Life is about more than food, and the body is about more than wearing clothes.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Look at the ravens. They don't sow or reap, they don't have any storerooms or barns, but God feeds them. And you're far more valuable than birds!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Can you add an hour to your life by worrying about it?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you can't do anything about such small things, why worry about the rest?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Think of the lilies and how they grow. They don't work, and they don't spin thread for clothes, but I tell you, not even Solomon in all his glory was as beautifully dressed as one of them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So if God clothes the fields with such beautiful flowers, which are here today but gone tomorrow when they are burned in a fire to heat an oven, how much more will God clothe you, you who have so little trust!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't be concerned about what you're going to eat or drink—don't worry about it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: These are all things that people in the world worry about, but your Father knows you need them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Search for God's kingdom, and you'll be given these things as well.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't be afraid, little flock, for your Father is happy to give you the kingdom.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Sell what you have, and give the money to the poor. Get yourselves purses that don't wear out: treasure in heaven that will never run out, where no thief can steal it and no moth can destroy it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For what you value the most shows who you really are.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Be dressed and ready, and keep your lamps lit,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: like servants waiting for their master when he returns from his wedding feast, prepared to open the door quickly for him when he comes and knocks.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How good it will be for those servants that the master finds watching when he returns. I tell you the truth, he will get dressed, have them sit down for a meal, and will come and serve them himself!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Even if he comes at midnight, or just before dawn—how good for them if he finds them watching and ready!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But remember this: if the master knew when a thief was coming, he would keep watch, and not allow his house to be broken into.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You must also be ready, for the Son of man is coming when you don't expect him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Is this story you're telling just for us, or for everyone?
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Peter asked.
The Lord replied, {
Who then is the trustworthy and wise manager, the one person in the household that the master puts in charge to share out their food at the right time?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: It will be good for that servant when his master returns and finds him doing what he should.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I tell you the truth, the master will put that servant in charge of everything.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But what if the servant were to say to himself, ‘My master is taking a long time in coming,’ and then starts beating the other servants, both men and women, feasting and getting drunk?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: That servant's master will return unexpectedly one day at a time he didn't anticipate, and will punish him severely, treating him as totally untrustworthy.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: That servant who knew what his master wanted and yet didn't get ready or follow his instructions, will be beaten severely;
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: but the servant who didn't know and did things deserving punishment will be beaten only lightly. From those who are given much, much will be required, and from those who are entrusted with more, more will be demanded.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I have come to set the earth on fire, and I really wish it was already burning!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But I have a baptism to go through, and I'm in agony, wishing it was over!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Do you think that I came to bring peace on earth? No, I tell you, I bring division.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: From now on, if there are five in a family, they will be divided against each other: three against two, and two against three.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: They will be divided against each other—father against son, son against father, mother against daughter, daughter against mother, mother-in-law against daughter-in-law, and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds. {
When you see a cloud rising in the west, you immediately say, ‘a rain-shower's coming,’ and it happens.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When a south wind blows, you say ‘it's going to be hot,’ and it is.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You hypocrites, how is it that you know how to rightly interpret the weather but you don't know how to interpret the present time?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Why don't you think for yourselves and judge what's the right thing to do?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As you go with your accuser to the magistrate, on the way you should be working on a settlement. Otherwise you may be dragged before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, you won't get out until you've paid the last penny.
segments.speaker: Jesus
